Let the following conditions be satisfied:

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]

\item $f = f(x)$ and $g = g(x)$ are continuous functions on $[0, 1]$,
\item there exists a number $a$ such that $0 < f(x) \le a < 1$ on $[0, 1]$,
\item there exists a number $u$ such that $\displaystyle{\max_{0 \le x \le 1}
      (g(x) + u f(x)) = u}$.

\end{enumerate}

Find constants $A$ and $B$ such that $F(x) = \dfrac{Ag(x)}{f(x) + B}$ is a
continuous function on $[0, 1]$ satisfying $\displaystyle{\max_{0 \le x \le 1}
F(x) = u}$, and prove that your function has the required properties.
